content_en:
If the husband has stipulated that the wife will not work, or if there was no condition at all, then the wife should listen to the husband.

It should be remembered that the wife should not jeopardise her marriage by working, merely to make a point. This is an issue which has to be worked out and agreed upon through discussions and mutual consultation.

b. The wife’s wealth and property.

Some men become greedy when they see that the wife has money. They force her to part with it. This is completely unlawful in Islam. The wife has absolute control and authority over her wealth and she may spend it accordingly.

The husband still has to provide for her even if she is wealthy.

This kind of unwarranted domination on the part of men leads to erasing any element of love between the pair. The magazine “Riyadh” has reported that a woman was forced to kill her husband, cut him into pieces and scatter them in two towns. This was because he used to hit her and demand money from her. Allah says:

“And give women their mahr as a gift. If they willing give you some portion of it, then receive it with an open heart.”**

Beauty plays a great role in creating a happy and prosperous marriage. Man, by nature, is lured towards beauty. This applies just as much to the man as to the woman. Many men feel that this is something that women like doing and they do not feel the necessity to dress up and look decent for their wives. This is totally wrong. Allah says:

“And they (the women) have just as much right, in kindness, as they have duties.”*

While it is true that men should not preen and become vain about minute details of their appearance, this does not mean that they should not make an effort to be more presentable. A man, who was grey, asked a lady’s hand in marriage. The lady said: “Tell him that I have grey hair.” The man immediately withdrew his bid. The lady then told everybody

>! Surah an-Nisa’ (4), ayat 4. * Surah al-Baqarah (2), ayat 228,

concerned that she had only black hair and she only wanted to show that women like to see what men like to see in their spouses.

Unfortunately, many women have an appalling attitude towards the whole concept of beauty and dressing up. They will remain in an absolute disgusting state for days on end. Then, when a social occasion arrives, they will dress up and present themselves with great pride and fuss. All this with the clothing and jewellery that their husbands bought for them. It is a tremendous injustice that they can decorate themselves for others but not for their own husbands. The husbands have more rights to their appearances and beauty than all other women combined.

I have been informed by many who read gossip magazines that the mother-in-law lives to destroy the marriage of her daughter and her happiness lies in teaching her daughter schemes and tricks whereby she is able to ruin the husband financially and physically.

Reality is quite different from that. The mother-in-law is someone who is well respected and sacrifices her happiness for the good of her daughter. But this does not mean that all mother-in-laws are like this. Some will be quite the opposite and that is within the realms of human possibility. But to assume that she is conniving all the time is an injustice. The same applies to the husband’s mother.

Committing sins in search for happiness.

This is extremely detrimental and can have grave repercussions. It has overtaken the minds of Muslims in the name of a “honeymoon”. In this month, the couple travel and commit so many sins in the hope of finding happiness. Nothing could be further from the Islamic spirit. In this month of “bliss”, they engage in so many un-Islamic activities. It is a month of “enjoyment” and thereafter a life of misery and pain. A Muslim’s whole life should be filled with happiness.

They look at pornographic films and magazines. They listen to erotic music or the very least is that the husband invites all his friends where he can show off his wife. Sins have no bliss attached to them, they come with trials and disasters. This is Allah’s Law and no one can escape His Law.

Perhaps the best issue to discuss at the end of this lecture is the issue of offering gifts to one another. Many fail to realise that this has a tremendous impact on marriage. This also applies to giving gifts to each other’s parents. Gifts get rid of resentments and dissolve hatred. This is why the Prophet (SAW) said: “Offer gifis to one another and you will increase love for one another.” ***

With gifts, there is spreading salaams (greetings) amongst each other and meeting one another with a smile and happy face. The Prophet (saw) said: “Shall I not inform you of something that will increase love amongst you if you do it. That is spread salaam.’*’ So try out giving gifts and spreading salaam. You will, Allah willing, see the result.

These are the most salient ingredients for the making of a happy marriage. Obviously, there has to be an ardent desire and diligent effort. This effort will form a believing family and raise generations from which leaders and pious personalities rise. The Muslim community is in dire need of confronting the evils of the enemy vis-a-vis the Muslim family.

The family which holds firm to their faith and practice and which makes the Qur’an and Sunnah a model for its life is more likely to exist in happiness and prosperity:

“So whoever follows My Guidance shall not roam astray, not become wretched.”

As for those who tum away from the right path; assume the characteristics of East and West blindly and raise their children on that standard, then they have only themselves to blame:

“Whoever turns away from My Remembrance shall experience a strained life. Besides, We shall resurrect him on the Day of Judgement while he is blind.”

Allah has promised great rewards and bounties for those couples who live according to his dictates:

“Together with their wives they shall recline under shady groves against cushions.”**

Angels will welcome and pray for them:

* Surah Taha (20), ayat 123. % Surah Taha (20), ayat 124. * Surah Ya Sin (36), ayat 52.

“O Our Lord! Let them enter gardens which You have promised them. (Let them be accompanied by) those among their parents, spouses and offspring who reformed. Only You are Mighty and Wise.”*”

On the other hand, those couples who do not abide by His

Commands should beware of being among those whom Allah describes in the verse:

“Gather those who are unjust with their spouses and whatever they worshipped besides Allah. Then lead them to the path of Hell.”**

Our final word is that All Praise is due to Allah, The Lord of the Worlds. Blessings be upon the messengers. May Allah send Salutations and Blessings on Muhammed, his family and his companions.

5’ Surah Ghafir (40), ayat 8. %* Surah as-Saffat (37), ayats 22-23

Examples for us from the women of the sahaba and the salaf-as-saliheen

* ‘Urwah said: “ ‘Aisha (RA) would not keep something of what Allah (SWT) provided her without giving it in charity.” 62

Nafeesah, daughter of al-Hasan, son of Zaid, son of alHasan ibn Ali, the grandson of the Prophet (SAW) was one of the righteous pious and devoted women. She used to spend the night in prayer as well as fast during the day. She would also cry a lot for fear of Allah. When she was told to take things easy and look after herself, she said: “How can I do that while there is in front of me an ascent which only the successful [people] can cross?”

She died while she was fasting. When she was asked to break her fast before her death she said : “How odd! I have been praying for thirty years to meet Allah (SWT) while I am fasting. Do you want me to break my fast now? This will never happen,” Then she passed away.

e¢ Maymmon ibn Mahraan said: Mu’awiyyah ibn Abu Sufyan (RA) proposed to Ummud-Dardaa’, but she refused to marry him, saying: ‘I heard Abu-Dardaa saying: Allah’s Messenger(SAW) said: “The woman will be given [in Jannah] to the last man she married [in this life}.””

e ‘Ikrimah said: “Asmaa’, daughter of Abu Bakr, was married to az-Zubair ibnul-‘Awwaam who was hard on her. She came to her father complaining to him about her husband, and he said, “ O my daughter! Be patient; for if a woman has a pious husband who dies before her and she does not marry any one after him, she will be reunited with him in Paradise.” 7
content_id:
Jika suami telah mensyaratkan agar istri tidak bekerja, atau jika tidak ada syarat sama sekali, maka istri hendaknya mendengarkan suami.

Perlu diingat bahwa istri tidak seharusnya membahayakan pernikahannya dengan bekerja, semata-mata untuk membuktikan sesuatu. Ini adalah masalah yang harus diselesaikan dan disepakati melalui diskusi dan musyawarah bersama.

b. Harta dan kekayaan istri.

Sebagian laki-laki menjadi tamak ketika melihat istrinya memiliki harta. Mereka memaksa istri untuk menyerahkannya. Ini sama sekali tidak dibenarkan dalam Islam. Istri memiliki kendali dan wewenang penuh atas hartanya dan ia boleh membelanjakannya sesuai kehendaknya.

Suami tetap wajib menafkahi istrinya meskipun istri adalah orang yang kaya.

Dominasi yang tidak beralasan seperti ini dari pihak laki-laki akan menghapus setiap unsur cinta di antara pasangan. Majalah "Riyadh" pernah melaporkan bahwa seorang wanita dipaksa membunuh suaminya, memotongnya menjadi potongan-potongan, dan menyebarkannya di dua kota. Ini karena suaminya sering memukulnya dan menuntut uang darinya. Allah berfirman:

"Dan berikanlah mahar kepada wanita sebagai pemberian. Jika mereka dengan sukarela memberikan sebagian darinya kepadamu, maka terimalah dengan hati yang lapang."**

Kecantikan memainkan peran besar dalam menciptakan pernikahan yang bahagia dan sejahtera. Manusia, secara fitrah, tertarik pada keindahan. Ini berlaku baik bagi laki-laki maupun wanita. Banyak laki-laki merasa bahwa berdandan adalah sesuatu yang disukai wanita dan mereka tidak merasa perlu untuk berpenampilan rapi dan pantas di hadapan istri mereka. Ini sama sekali keliru. Allah berfirman:

"Dan mereka (para wanita) memiliki hak yang setara, secara ma'ruf, sebagaimana mereka memiliki kewajiban."*

Meskipun benar bahwa laki-laki tidak seharusnya bersolek berlebihan dan menjadi sombong terhadap detail-detail kecil penampilan mereka, ini tidak berarti mereka tidak perlu berusaha untuk berpenampilan lebih baik. Seorang laki-laki yang sudah beruban melamar seorang wanita. Wanita itu berkata: "Katakan kepadanya bahwa saya juga beruban." Laki-laki itu segera menarik lamarannya. Wanita itu kemudian memberitahu semua orang

>! Surah an-Nisa' (4), ayat 4. * Surah al-Baqarah (2), ayat 228,

yang bersangkutan bahwa rambutnya sebenarnya hitam semua dan ia hanya ingin menunjukkan bahwa wanita juga ingin melihat apa yang ingin dilihat laki-laki pada pasangan mereka.

Sayangnya, banyak wanita memiliki sikap yang buruk terhadap konsep kecantikan dan berdandan. Mereka akan tetap dalam keadaan yang sangat tidak terawat berhari-hari lamanya. Kemudian, ketika ada acara sosial, mereka akan berdandan dan menampilkan diri dengan penuh kebanggaan dan kesibukan. Semua itu dengan pakaian dan perhiasan yang dibeli oleh suami mereka. Sungguh ketidakadilan yang besar bahwa mereka bisa mempercantik diri untuk orang lain tetapi tidak untuk suami mereka sendiri. Suami memiliki hak yang lebih besar atas penampilan dan kecantikan mereka daripada semua wanita lain digabungkan.

Saya telah diberitahu oleh banyak orang yang membaca majalah gosip bahwa mertua perempuan hidup untuk menghancurkan pernikahan putrinya dan kebahagiaannya terletak pada mengajarkan putrinya tipu muslihat dan trik yang dengannya ia dapat meruntuhkan suami secara finansial dan fisik.

Kenyataannya sangat berbeda dari itu. Mertua perempuan adalah seseorang yang sangat dihormati dan mengorbankan kebahagiaannya demi kebaikan putrinya. Tetapi ini tidak berarti semua mertua perempuan seperti itu. Sebagian akan sangat bertolak belakang dan itu masih dalam kemungkinan manusiawi. Tetapi menganggap bahwa ia selalu bersekongkol adalah suatu ketidakadilan. Hal yang sama berlaku untuk ibu dari pihak suami.

Melakukan dosa dalam mencari kebahagiaan.

Ini sangat merugikan dan dapat menimbulkan dampak yang serius. Hal ini telah menguasai pikiran umat Islam dengan nama "bulan madu". Pada bulan ini, pasangan bepergian dan melakukan begitu banyak dosa dengan harapan menemukan kebahagiaan. Tidak ada yang lebih jauh dari semangat Islam. Pada bulan "kebahagiaan" ini, mereka terlibat dalam begitu banyak aktivitas yang tidak Islami. Itu adalah bulan "kesenangan" dan setelahnya kehidupan penuh kesengsaraan dan penderitaan. Seluruh kehidupan seorang Muslim seharusnya dipenuhi dengan kebahagiaan.

Mereka menonton film dan majalah pornografi. Mereka mendengarkan musik erotis atau paling tidak suami mengundang semua temannya di mana ia bisa memamerkan istrinya. Dosa tidak membawa kebahagiaan; dosa datang bersama ujian dan musibah. Ini adalah hukum Allah dan tidak ada seorang pun yang dapat lolos dari hukum-Nya.

Mungkin masalah terbaik untuk dibahas di akhir ceramah ini adalah masalah saling memberi hadiah. Banyak yang gagal menyadari bahwa hal ini memiliki dampak yang luar biasa terhadap pernikahan. Ini juga berlaku untuk memberi hadiah kepada orang tua masing-masing. Hadiah menghilangkan rasa dendam dan melarutkan kebencian. Inilah mengapa Nabi (SAW) bersabda: "Saling memberi hadiahlah kalian, niscaya kalian akan menambah rasa cinta di antara kalian." ***

Bersama dengan hadiah, ada penyebaran salam di antara satu sama lain dan bertemu satu sama lain dengan senyuman dan wajah yang ceria. Nabi (saw) bersabda: "Maukah kalian aku beritahu tentang sesuatu yang akan menambah cinta di antara kalian jika kalian melakukannya? Yaitu sebarkan salam."' Maka cobalah memberi hadiah dan menyebarkan salam. Kalian akan, insya Allah, melihat hasilnya.

Inilah unsur-unsur terpenting untuk membentuk pernikahan yang bahagia. Tentu saja, harus ada keinginan yang kuat dan usaha yang tekun. Usaha ini akan membentuk keluarga yang beriman dan membesarkan generasi-generasi yang darinya muncul pemimpin dan pribadi-pribadi yang saleh. Komunitas Muslim sangat membutuhkan untuk menghadapi kejahatan musuh terhadap keluarga Muslim.

Keluarga yang berpegang teguh pada iman dan amalan mereka serta menjadikan al-Quran dan Sunnah sebagai pedoman hidupnya lebih mungkin hidup dalam kebahagiaan dan kesejahteraan:

"Maka barangsiapa yang mengikuti petunjuk-Ku, ia tidak akan tersesat dan tidak akan celaka."

Adapun mereka yang berpaling dari jalan yang benar; mengambil karakteristik Timur dan Barat secara membabi buta dan membesarkan anak-anak mereka dengan standar itu, maka mereka hanya bisa menyalahkan diri sendiri:

"Barangsiapa yang berpaling dari peringatan-Ku, maka sungguh ia akan mengalami kehidupan yang sempit. Selain itu, Kami akan membangkitkannya pada Hari Kiamat dalam keadaan buta."

Allah telah menjanjikan pahala dan karunia yang besar bagi pasangan-pasangan yang hidup sesuai dengan perintah-perintah-Nya:

"Bersama istri-istri mereka, mereka akan bersandar di bawah naungan yang rindang di atas dipan-dipan."**

Para malaikat akan menyambut dan mendoakan mereka:

* Surah Taha (20), ayat 123. % Surah Taha (20), ayat 124. * Surah Ya Sin (36), ayat 52.

"Wahai Tuhan kami! Masukkanlah mereka ke dalam surga yang telah Engkau janjikan kepada mereka. (Hendaklah mereka disertai oleh) orang-orang yang saleh di antara orang tua, pasangan, dan keturunan mereka. Hanya Engkaulah Yang Mahaperkasa lagi Mahabijaksana."*"

Di sisi lain, pasangan-pasangan yang tidak mematuhi

perintah-perintah-Nya hendaknya berhati-hati agar tidak termasuk di antara mereka yang Allah sebutkan dalam ayat:

"Kumpulkanlah orang-orang yang zalim bersama pasangan-pasangan mereka dan apa yang dahulu mereka sembah selain Allah. Kemudian tunjukkanlah kepada mereka jalan menuju Neraka."**

Kata terakhir kami adalah bahwa segala puji bagi Allah, Tuhan seluruh alam. Shalawat dan salam atas para rasul. Semoga Allah melimpahkan shalawat dan salam kepada Muhammad, keluarga, dan para sahabatnya.

5' Surah Ghafir (40), ayat 8. %* Surah as-Saffat (37), ayat 22-23

Teladan bagi kita dari para wanita sahabat dan salaf as-saliheen

* 'Urwah berkata: " 'Aisyah (RA) tidak pernah menyimpan sesuatu dari apa yang Allah (SWT) berikan kepadanya tanpa menyedekahkannya." 62

Nafeesah, putri al-Hasan, putra Zaid, putra al-Hasan ibn Ali, cucu Nabi (SAW) adalah salah satu wanita yang salehah, bertakwa, dan tekun beribadah. Ia menghabiskan malam untuk shalat dan berpuasa di siang hari. Ia juga banyak menangis karena takut kepada Allah. Ketika ia diminta untuk memudahkan diri dan menjaga kesehatannya, ia berkata: "Bagaimana aku bisa melakukan itu sementara di hadapanku ada pendakian yang hanya bisa dilalui oleh orang-orang yang beruntung?"

Ia wafat dalam keadaan berpuasa. Ketika ia diminta untuk berbuka sebelum wafatnya, ia berkata: "Sungguh aneh! Aku telah berdoa selama tiga puluh tahun untuk bertemu Allah (SWT) dalam keadaan berpuasa. Apakah kalian ingin aku berbuka sekarang? Itu tidak akan pernah terjadi." Kemudian ia pun meninggal dunia.

Maymoon ibn Mahraan berkata: Mu'awiyyah ibn Abu Sufyan (RA) melamar Ummud-Dardaa', tetapi ia menolak menikahinya, seraya berkata: 'Aku mendengar Abu Dardaa' berkata: Rasulullah (SAW) bersabda: "Seorang wanita akan diberikan [di surga] kepada suami terakhir yang menikahinya [di dunia]."'

'Ikrimah berkata: "Asma', putri Abu Bakar, menikah dengan az-Zubair ibnul-'Awwaam yang keras terhadapnya. Ia datang kepada ayahnya mengadukan suaminya, dan ayahnya berkata, 'Wahai putriku! Bersabarlah; karena jika seorang wanita memiliki suami yang saleh yang meninggal sebelumnya dan ia tidak menikah lagi setelahnya, ia akan dipertemukan kembali dengannya di surga.'" 7

If the husband has stipulated that the wife will not work, or if there was no condition at all, then the wife should listen to the husband.

It should be remembered that the wife should not jeopardise her marriage by working, merely to make a point. This is an issue which has to be worked out and agreed upon through discussions and mutual consultation.

Some men become greedy when they see that the wife has money. They force her to part with it. This is completely unlawful in Islam. The wife has absolute control and authority over her wealth and she may spend it accordingly.

The husband still has to provide for her even if she is wealthy.

>! Surah an-Nisa’ (4), ayat 4. * Surah al-Baqarah (2), ayat 228,

concerned that she had only black hair and she only wanted to show that women like to see what men like to see in their spouses.

Unfortunately, many women have an appalling attitude towards the whole concept of beauty and dressing up. They will remain in an absolute disgusting state for days on end. Then, when a social occasion arrives, they will dress up and present themselves with great pride and fuss. All this with the clothing and jewellery that their husbands bought for them. It is a tremendous injustice that they can decorate themselves for others but not for their own husbands. The husbands have more rights to their appearances and beauty than all other women combined.

I have been informed by many who read gossip magazines that the mother-in-law lives to destroy the marriage of her daughter and her happiness lies in teaching her daughter schemes and tricks whereby she is able to ruin the husband financially and physically.

This is extremely detrimental and can have grave repercussions. It has overtaken the minds of Muslims in the name of a “honeymoon”. In this month, the couple travel and commit so many sins in the hope of finding happiness. Nothing could be further from the Islamic spirit. In this month of “bliss”, they engage in so many un-Islamic activities. It is a month of “enjoyment” and thereafter a life of misery and pain. A Muslim’s whole life should be filled with happiness.

They look at pornographic films and magazines. They listen to erotic music or the very least is that the husband invites all his friends where he can show off his wife. Sins have no bliss attached to them, they come with trials and disasters. This is الله’s Law and no one can escape His Law.

Perhaps the best issue to discuss at the end of this lecture is the issue of offering gifts to one another. Many fail to realise that this has a tremendous impact on marriage. This also applies to giving gifts to each other’s parents. Gifts get rid of resentments and dissolve hatred. With gifts, there is spreading salaams (greetings) amongst each other and meeting one another with a smile and happy face. النبي ﷺ (saw) said: “Shall I not inform you of something that will increase love amongst you if you do it. That is spread salaam.’*’ So try out giving gifts and spreading salaam. You will, الله willing, see the result.

These are the most salient ingredients for the making of a happy marriage. Obviously, there has to be an ardent desire and diligent effort. This effort will form a believing family and raise generations from which leaders and pious personalities rise. The Muslim community is in dire need of confronting the evils of the enemy vis-a-vis the Muslim family.

The family which holds firm to their faith and practice and which makes the Qur’an and السنة النبوية a model for its life is more likely to exist in happiness and prosperity:

“So whoever follows My Guidance shall not roam astray, not become wretched.”

As for those who tum away from the right path; assume the characteristics of East and West blindly and raise their children on that standard, then they have only themselves to blame:

“Whoever turns away from My Remembrance shall experience a strained life. Besides, We shall resurrect him on the Day of Judgement while he is blind.”

الله has promised great rewards and bounties for those couples who live according to his dictates:

“Together with their wives they shall recline under shady groves against cushions.”**

Angels will welcome and pray for them:

* Surah Taha (20), ayat 123. % Surah Taha (20), ayat 124. * Surah Ya Sin (36), ayat 52.

“O Our Lord! Let them enter gardens which You have promised them. (Let them be accompanied by) those among their parents, spouses and offspring who reformed. Only You are Mighty and Wise.”*”

On the other hand, those couples who do not abide by His

Commands should beware of being among those whom الله describes in the verse:

“Gather those who are unjust with their spouses and whatever they worshipped besides الله. Then lead them to the path of Hell.”**

Our final word is that All Praise is due to الله, The Lord of the Worlds. Blessings be upon the messengers. May الله send Salutations and Blessings on Muhammed, his family and his الصحابة.

5’ Surah Ghafir (40), ayat 8. %* Surah as-Saffat (37), ayats 22-23

Examples for us from the women of the sahaba and the salaf-as-saliheen

* ‘Urwah said: “ ‘Aisha (RA) would not keep something of what الله (SWT) provided her without giving it in charity.” 62

Nafeesah, daughter of al-Hasan, son of Zaid, son of alHasan ibn Ali, the grandson of النبي ﷺ (SAW) was one of the righteous pious and devoted women. She used to spend the night in prayer as well as fast during the day. She would also cry a lot for fear of الله. When she was told to take things easy and look after herself, she said: “How can I do that while there is in front of me an ascent which only the successful [people] can cross?”

She died while she was fasting. When she was asked to break her fast before her death she said : “How odd! I have been praying for thirty years to meet الله (SWT) while I am fasting. Do you want me to break my fast now? This will never happen,” Then she passed away.

e¢ Maymmon ibn Mahraan said: Mu’awiyyah ibn Abu Sufyan (RA) proposed to Ummud-Dardaa’, but she refused to marry him, saying: ‘I heard Abu-Dardaa saying: الله’s Messenger(SAW) said: “The woman will be given [in Jannah] to the last man she married [in this life}.””

e ‘Ikrimah said: “Asmaa’, daughter of Abu Bakr, was married to az-Zubair ibnul-‘Awwaam who was hard on her. She came to her father complaining to him about her husband, and he said, “ O my daughter! Be patient; for if a woman has a pious husband who dies before her and she does not marry any one after him, she will be reunited with him in Paradise.” 7
